document.title: Risk Management Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector
authorityOrJurisdiction: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for the ADD Abyz Digital Detector, File No. A-TCF-5-037 Rev.0.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management planning, hazard identification, risk evaluation, risk control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information for the ADD medical device.
keyPoints: The ADD is a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
Risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2012 and reference IEC 60601-1 and other medical device standards and EU directives.
The ADD is designed for an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Risk evaluation identified 30 hazards and all were listed as unacceptable before risk control.
Residual risk evaluation lists 30 controlled hazards and all were judged acceptable.
The conclusion states that the risk management plan was appropriately implemented, overall residual risk is acceptable, and methods are in place for production and post-production information.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2012

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Expected service life
concepts.evidence: The ADD is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.

concepts.evidence: Green Zone Acceptable Region Risk Level: 1~4 Red Zone Unacceptable Region Risk Level: 5~25

concepts.name: Residual risk acceptability
concepts.evidence: Result: Overall residual risks are minimized, therefore, it is considered to be acceptable

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall establish generic procedures to collect information from various sources

evidence.claim: The document is a Risk Management Report for the ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector Report No.: A-TCF-5-037 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The product name is Abyz Digital Detector and model name is ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name: Abyz Digital Detector Model Name: ADD

evidence.claim: ADD is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: ADD is described as a portable digital X-ray flat panel wireless detector.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is a portable digital X-ray flat panel wireless detector that can generate Images of any part of the body.

evidence.claim: The ADD system includes an X-ray detector, power supply box, battery recharger, switch box, battery packs, and main cable.
evidence.evidenceText: This X-ray imaging system consists of an X-ray detector (an a-Si TFT sensor panel with scintillator), a power supply box, a battery recharger, switch box, battery packs and a main cable.

evidence.claim: The report states that risk management methods and procedures focus on EN ISO 14971:2012 and EN 60601-1:2006/A1:2013.
evidence.evidenceText: Main methods and procedures we selected herewith are focused on EN ISO 14971:2012 and EN 60601-1:2006/A1:2013.

evidence.claim: The ADD is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as Severity x Frequency.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level = Severity x Frequency

evidence.claim: The acceptability table defines risk level 1 to 4 as acceptable and 5 to 25 as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone Acceptable Region Risk Level: 1~4 Red Zone Unacceptable Region Risk Level: 5~25

evidence.claim: Risk evaluation identified 30 hazards and zero acceptable hazards before risk control.
evidence.evidenceText: Total 30 - 30

evidence.claim: Residual risk evaluation records 30 controlled hazards and 30 acceptable residual risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Total 30 30 - -

evidence.claim: Completeness of risk control was evaluated as acceptable for 30 identified hazards and 30 risk control measures.
evidence.evidenceText: Total 30 30 30 - Acceptable

evidence.claim: The overall residual risk result states that residual risks are minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Result: Overall residual risks are minimized, therefore, it is considered to be acceptable

evidence.claim: The conclusion states that the overall residual risk is acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Through overall residual risk assessment, we are confirmed that overall of the residual risk can be accepted.

evidence.claim: The quality management system is based on EN ISO13485: 2016.
evidence.evidenceText: Quality management system based on EN ISO13485: 2016.

evidence.claim: No products had been sold yet as of the collect-of-information section.
evidence.evidenceText: No products have been sold yet.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is ambiguous: the header and approval table use JUN/15/2020, while the conclusion section lists Date: 2020.07.22.
Authority or jurisdiction is not stated as an issuing authority; EU directives are referenced standards/regulatory instruments in the document.
Source text contains OCR or formatting artifacts such as unusual apostrophes, split standard names, and typographical errors, but the main metadata and technical claims are readable.
